page: Microsoft Azure M32bds_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8473C processor running at 4.0 GHz. It features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading, and 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The hardware configuration includes a 410 GB local storage disk and supports Premium Storage. The processor incorporates advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and single-core performance. The server achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth is strong at 181.54 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput of 52.78 GB/sec. However, while small-model LLM text generation is strong, large-model text generation is a performance bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25% at 2.74 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M32bds_v3 offers a balanced resource density for memory-bound workloads without requiring expensive GPU hardware. The combination of high memory capacity, dedicated CPU allocation, and local storage makes it suitable for database operations, memory-intensive web serving, and text processing pipelines. It is less optimal for heavy parallelized graphics rendering or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure M32bds_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8473C processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 4.0 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, a 410 GB local storage disk, and supports Premium Storage. The instance excels in memory-intensive and single-core tasks, achieving top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks, alongside strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 181.54 GB/sec. While it delivers strong performance in static web serving and small-model LLM text generation, it exhibits weak performance in large-model LLM inference due to the absence of GPU acceleration. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads, making it highly suitable for database operations, text processing, and memory-intensive web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M32bds_v3 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 410 GB local disk. It features an Intel Xeon 8473C CPU, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M32bds_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 410 GB local disk powered by an Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, 256 GB RAM, and local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8473C processor with 32 vCPUs and 4.0 GHz clock speed.
256.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio.
410 GB local storage disk with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmark performance.
Strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 181.54 GB/sec.
Best suited for database operations, text processing, and memory-intensive web serving.
